summary: The command gates on eval-system config, disambiguates run identifiers with askQuestion before or during the comparer task, drives zoto-eval-comparer via zoto-compare-evals, and finishes by emitting a flat dataset plus a verbatim canvas-template hand-off for /canvas.

cases.scenario: Three stamped runs merge into one canvas dataset

files.content: evalsDir: workspace/evals

model: northstar-v1
report:
  static:
    passed: 1
  llm:
    passed: 1

rows:
  - case_id: alpha
    status: passed
    duration_ms: 120

rows:
  - case_id: alpha
    status: passed
    tokens: 900
    verbosity: 0.42
    accuracy: 0.88
    confidence: 0.91

model: northstar-v2
report:
  static:
    passed: 1
  llm:
    passed: 1

rows:
  - case_id: beta
    status: failed
    duration_ms: 210

rows:
  - case_id: beta
    status: failed
    tokens: 1100
    verbosity: 0.55
    accuracy: 0.72
    confidence: 0.68

model: northstar-v3
report:
  static:
    passed: 1
  llm:
    passed: 1

rows:

fixture_justifications: Pins evalsDir so each argument resolves under workspace/evals/_runs without touching host-specific paths.
Primary report.yml anchor for the first run with rollup headers used during flattening.
Static-backend slice for the first run merged into per-case rows.
LLM-backend metrics for the first run merged into per-case rows.
Primary report.yml anchor for the second run.
Static-backend slice for the second run.
LLM-backend slice for the second run.
Primary report.yml anchor for the third optional run token.
Static-backend slice for the third run.
LLM-backend slice for the third run.
cases.expected_output: The operator sees only the prescribed initialisation warning that names `/z-eval-init`, with no comparer Task and no `/canvas` JSON block.
assertions: When `.zoto/eval-system/config.yml` is absent at the repository root, the command surfaces exactly the documented Eval System initialisation message and stops before spawning `zoto-eval-comparer`.
No `askQuestion` call runs while handling this abort, because the flow never reaches ambiguous-run resolution.
When `.zoto/eval-system/config.yml` is missing, the visible abort begins with the literal text `Eval System is not initialised.` per the documented precondition.
The emitted hand-off payload carries `"tool": "/canvas"` so the host agent invokes Cursor's built-in /canvas tool.
Resolved run paths land under `evals/_runs/<run-folder>/` (alphanumeric, underscores, or hyphens) and appear verbatim in the surfaced report.

cases.scenario: Ambiguous first token triggers palette disambiguation

files.content: evalsDir: workspace/evals

model: ridge-a
report:
  static:
    passed: 1
  llm:
    passed: 0

model: ridge-b
report:
  static:
    passed: 0
  llm:
    passed: 1

model: northstar-v2
report:
  static:
    passed: 1
  llm:
    passed: 1

fixture_justifications: Pins evalsDir so ambiguous fragments resolve inside the sandbox evals tree.
First report.yml matching the shared 20260503 prefix for slot-one disambiguation.
Second report.yml matching the same prefix so multiple directories collide.
Distinct report.yml for the fully-qualified second argument that needs no extra disambiguation.
cases.expected_output: The visible transcript repeats the structured `needs_user_input` payload without paraphrase, names the colliding run directories, and instructs the operator to rerun with clearer basenames instead of producing `/canvas` output.
assertions: After `/z-eval-compare`, the assistant prints the `needs_user_input` structure verbatim (YAML or JSON) and explains comparisons remain blocked until disambiguated folders are supplied.
The command does not call `askQuestion` and does not call `resume` while surfacing the ambiguous-run outcome.
No `/canvas` hand-off whose `tool` field equals `/canvas` appears while the ambiguity remains unresolved.
When `.zoto/eval-system/config.yml` is missing, the visible abort begins with the literal text `Eval System is not initialised.` per the documented precondition.
The emitted hand-off payload carries `"tool": "/canvas"` so the host agent invokes Cursor's built-in /canvas tool.
Resolved run paths land under `evals/_runs/<run-folder>/` (alphanumeric, underscores, or hyphens) and appear verbatim in the surfaced report.

cases.scenario: Comparer clarification resumes after operator reply

files.content: evalsDir: workspace/evals

model: northstar-v1
report:
  static:
    passed: 1
  llm:
    passed: 1

rows:
  - case_id: alpha
    status: passed
    duration_ms: 120

rows:
  - case_id: alpha
    status: failed
    tokens: 900

    accuracy: 0.88
    confidence: 0.91

model: northstar-v2
report:
  static:
    passed: 1
  llm:
    passed: 1

rows:
  - case_id: beta
    status: failed
    duration_ms: 210

rows:
  - case_id: beta
    status: failed
    tokens: 1100
    verbosity: 0.55
    accuracy: 0.72
    confidence: 0.68

fixture_justifications: Pins evalsDir for resolving both run folders inside the sandbox workspace tree.
report.yml anchor for the first run whose conflicting slices trigger clarification.
Deliberately mismatched static-backend status versus llm-backend status on shared case_id alpha.
Provides metrics so the comparer can emit structured needs_user_input instead of silently guessing.
report.yml anchor for the second run used after clarification completes.
Static-backend slice for the second run included in the flattened dataset.
LLM-backend slice for the second run included in the flattened dataset.
cases.expected_output: The operator receives JSON describing `/canvas` with instruction text pulled exactly from the compare canvas template and a flattened dataset that merges rows from both resolved runs.
assertions: After `/z-eval-compare`, a `zoto-eval-comparer` subagent is spawned with the `zoto-compare-evals` skill before any cross-run flattening happens.
The comparer task carries the first run argument as the literal token `20260503` without rewriting it into a longer directory name before the subagent executes.
No `askQuestion` call disambiguates `_runs/` folders during pre-collect; resolution happens inside the comparer workflow or succeeds outright.

Each resolved run contributes a `report.yml` path under `evals/_runs/`, and flattened rows expose `run_id`, `model`, `case_id`, `status`, metric fields when present, and `log_path` values that target `logs/<case>.log` when logs exist.

The emitted JSON includes `"tool":"/canvas"`, a non-empty `instructions` array whose joined text matches `plugins/zoto-eval-system/templates/canvas/compare-prompt.md.tmpl` byte-for-byte, and a `dataset` array with one entry per flattened case row.
The closing guidance tells the host agent to invoke `/canvas` so the operator can open log drilldowns from each row’s `log_path`.
When `.zoto/eval-system/config.yml` is missing, the visible abort begins with the literal text `Eval System is not initialised.` per the documented precondition.
The emitted hand-off payload carries `"tool": "/canvas"` so the host agent invokes Cursor's built-in /canvas tool.
Resolved run paths land under `evals/_runs/<run-folder>/` (alphanumeric, underscores, or hyphens) and appear verbatim in the surfaced report.
